title: Legion
subtitle: The two demoniacs of Gerasa, who cannot bear the presence of Christ

stillness: Be still before the One whose mere presence is torment to the demons.
For, as Jerome says, the presence of the Saviour is the torment of devils.
Quiet your heart, and let that same Presence draw near to you.
gospel: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed over,
and passed the sea of Galilee,
they came into the region of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
And when he had gone out of the boat onto the land,
there met him two men possessed by demons, exceedingly fierce,
coming out of the tombs, where they dwelt and had their home;
who were bound with chains and fetters,
and, the bonds being broken, were driven by the demons into the desert,
so that no one could pass that way.
And feeling the power of God, falling down before Jesus, they adored him,
and cried out with a loud voice, saying:
What have we to do with thee, Jesus, Son of God?
Thou hast come here before the appointed time to torment us.
And Jesus asked them their name.
And they said: Legion is my name, for we are many.
And they begged him not to cast them out of the region into the abyss,
but to let them enter into the swine.
And he permitted them. And the herd of swine, two thousand,
rushed into the lake of Genesareth and were drowned;
and so the demoniacs were freed and healed.
behold: Behold the two men possessed by demons, exceedingly fierce,
for they raged against themselves and against others.
They come out of the tombs, where they dwelt and had their home.
They were bound with chains and fetters,
and, the bonds broken, were driven by the demons into the desert.
See: no one could pass that way —
for this the raging demons intend, to block for us the way of life.
And behold, feeling the power of God,
and out of great fear, not out of humility,
they fall down before Jesus and adore him,
and cry out, being compelled, with a loud voice.
consider: This is not the confession of the will, but the wresting of necessity, for they fear his presence.
What have we to do with thee? As if they said: nothing is common to thee and to us.
Thou art God, we are devils; thou art humble, we are proud; thou hast come to save, we to destroy.
For what concord hath Christ with Belial? None, surely.
Christ does all things well; the devil does all things ill.
Punishment opens the eyes that guilt closes.
They were scourged invisibly, pierced and transfixed, suffering things unbearable from his presence alone.
For it is great torment to the demons to cease from harming men, because of the hatred they bear toward us;
and the longer they have possessed, the more grievously they let go.
Legion is their name, for they are many — a legion numbers six thousand six hundred and sixty-six.
Behold the weakness of the demons: they can do nothing unless permitted.
If they could not harm the swine unless permitted, how much less men, made to the image of God.
Therefore God alone must be feared, and these despised.
See their vileness: they wish to enter into swine — into an unclean animal.
Like these two demoniacs are they who serve God from fear of hell rather than love of justice.
Let him who is wounded by the devil break his yoke quickly;
for the sooner his dominion is shunned, the more easily it is escaped.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, most kind lover of men,
I beseech thy goodness with tears:
free me from all the filth of my sins,
and from the assaults of the demons,
and, having freed me from them, mercifully keep me unharmed until the end,
that to thy glory and the good of my neighbours
I may be able to tell in words and show in works
what great things thou hast done for my soul, set free by thy grace;
so that others, knowing that this is the change of the right hand of the Most High,
may all be moved by my example,
and, by thy mercy, be converted to better things. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Non est voluntatis confessio, sed necessitatis extorsio, quia timent ejus præsentiam.
latinJewel.english: It is not the confession of the will, but the wresting of necessity, for they fear his presence.
